bids.reasoning: As this is the opening offer at the very beginning of the negotiation, I will start by proposing my most preferred values for all issues. This serves to maximize my potential utility and sets a strong initial anchor from which I can steadily make considered concessions in subsequent rounds.

The negotiation has just started (time fraction 0.02) and the opponent has countered with a bid that maximizes their utility at the cost of mine. To show a willingness to negotiate while prioritizing my highest-weighted issues, I will maintain my positions on issueD (weight 0.4), issueC (weight 0.3), and issueB (weight 0.2) and make a minor concession on issueA (weight 0.1). This step starts the concession process cautiously.

We are very early in the negotiation (t = 0.04) and the opponent has simply repeated their opening offer of all least-preferred values for me. To keep the negotiation moving, I will incrementally concede on my lowest-priority issue (issueA) while holding firm on my most important issues (issueC and issueD) to protect my overall utility and signal my core priorities.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.06). The opponent has made a major concession on issueD (my highest-weight issue) to my most preferred value 'valueF', and practically half-conceded on issueC to 'valueD'. I will solidify the progress on issueD and keep pushing for valueF on issueC. Meanwhile, I will continue to make small concessions on my least important issues (issueA and issueB) to maintain momentum and encourage them to fully concede on issueC.

The negotiation is still very early (t=0.08). The opponent has offered my top-preferred outcome on my most important issue (issueD: valueF), which is an excellent starting point. I will match their latest offer on my lowest-priority issue, issueA, by offering valueB, and make another minor concession on issueB (to valueD) while holding firm on my highest-priority issues (issueC and issueD) to maintain strong utility.
